name: Radio
description: Radio picks exactly one choice from a small, mutually exclusive set. It's almost never used alone — it's meant to be composed inside @mui/material/RadioGroup (also a plain karoo-ui re-export, see lib/RadioGroup/index.tsx), which supplies `name`, `value`, and `onChange` to every descendant Radio via context so only one can ever be checked.
keyRule: Use Radio (inside a RadioGroup) for one choice from a short set (2–5). Beyond ~5 options, use a select/menu instead. Don't use it for independent multi-select (use Checkbox) or an instant on/off setting (use Switch).
correction: One of three real components split out of the old MDC-modeled 'SelectionControl' umbrella, which documented Checkbox, Radio, and Switch behind a single fake `type` discriminator prop and gave each a manual `name` prop for grouping. The real grouping mechanism is a dedicated pairing component, RadioGroup — not a shared string prop the developer has to keep in sync by hand.

variants.name: Grouped (RadioGroup) — the standard pattern
variants.whenToUse: Wrap a set of Radios in <RadioGroup name="..." value={...} onChange={...}>, each Radio paired with FormControlLabel for its label. This is how mutual exclusivity is actually enforced.

variants.name: Standalone Radio
variants.whenToUse: Rare — a single Radio outside a RadioGroup has no innate awareness of other options; you'd have to wire `checked`/`onChange`/shared `name` manually across each instance. Prefer RadioGroup even for a small manually-laid-out set.

anatomy.name: RadioGroup (real pairing component)
anatomy.description: the container that owns the group's value/name/onChange and provides them to descendant Radios via context; not part of Radio itself.

anatomy.name: Root
anatomy.description: a ButtonBase-backed clickable area (renders as a button element) containing the visible icon and a visually-hidden native `<input type="radio">`.

anatomy.name: Icon (unchecked)
anatomy.description: default RadioButtonUnchecked icon; override via the `icon` prop.

anatomy.name: Label (external)
anatomy.description: not part of Radio itself. Wrap each Radio in @mui/material/FormControlLabel for a clickable label — the old SelectionControl's built-in `label` prop has no real equivalent.

anatomy.name: Group label (external)
anatomy.description: typically a FormLabel/FormControl wrapping the whole RadioGroup, giving the set an accessible group name.

behaviors.name: Group-driven selection
behaviors.description: RadioGroup clones its Radio children (via FormControlLabel's `control`) and injects `checked` (by comparing each Radio's `value` to the group's `value`), `name`, and `onChange`. This is what actually enforces 'only one active' — Radio itself has no exclusivity logic.

behaviors.name: Controlled vs uncontrolled group
behaviors.description: RadioGroup takes `value`+`onChange` (controlled) or `defaultValue` (uncontrolled) at the group level, not per-Radio.

behaviors.name: Apply timing
behaviors.description: Not enforced by the component. A radio group's selection usually applies when the surrounding form is submitted, but that's a decision made by the form, not Radio.

states.treatment: Primary-coloured filled circle icon (color="primary" by default).
ButtonBase ripple/state-layer around the icon; visible focus ring.
Theme disabled-action opacity; not focusable. Can be set per-Radio or via RadioGroup context on the whole group.

specs.value: size="small" — set at the theme level (theme.ts: MuiRadio.defaultProps.size), not by this wrapper. MUI's own default is "medium".
medium ≈24px icon with a larger click/touch target via internal padding; small (Cartrack default) ≈20px icon — MUI internals, not tokenized.
Radio only supports 'small' | 'medium' — unlike Checkbox/Switch, MUI Radio has no 'large' size.

props.name: checked
props.description: Controlled checked state. In practice usually supplied by RadioGroup, not set directly.

props.name: defaultChecked
props.description: Uncontrolled initial checked state (rare — RadioGroup's own defaultValue is the normal way to seed a group).

props.name: onChange
props.description: Fired when this Radio becomes checked. Usually supplied by RadioGroup rather than set per-Radio.

props.name: size
props.description: Default 'medium' in stock MUI, overridden to 'small' fleet-wide by theme.ts.

props.name: required
props.description: Marks the underlying native input as required.

props.name: name
props.description: Name attribute of the native input. Normally set once on RadioGroup and propagated to every Radio in the set, not per-Radio.

props.name: value
props.description: This Radio's value within the group — compared against RadioGroup's `value` to determine its checked state.

props.name: id
props.description: id of the native input — pair with FormControlLabel's htmlFor.

doThis: Wrap every set of Radios in a RadioGroup so mutual exclusivity is real, not manually simulated.
Use FormControlLabel to give every Radio a clickable label.
Give the whole group an accessible name (FormLabel above the RadioGroup, or aria-label/aria-labelledby).
dontDoThis: Don't use more than ~5 Radios — switch to a select/menu.
Don't wire `checked`/`onChange`/shared `name` manually across standalone Radios when RadioGroup already does this correctly.
Don't use a Switch where users expect a deliberate, submitted choice from options.
accessibility: Renders a real native `<input type="radio">` (visually hidden) so screen readers get correct semantics for free.
RadioGroup should be wrapped in a FormControl with a FormLabel (or use role="radiogroup" + aria-label) so the set has an accessible group name.
Always pair each Radio with FormControlLabel (or your own <label htmlFor>) so its option text is announced and clickable.
Focus is visible via the theme's focus ring; arrow keys move selection within a native radio group as usual.

related.name: RadioGroup
related.why: The real pairing component — manages shared name/value/onChange across a set of Radios so only one is selected; almost always used together

related.name: Checkbox
related.why: Independent multi-select — split from the same old SelectionControl umbrella

related.name: Switch
related.why: Instant on/off setting — split from the same old SelectionControl umbrella
